title: fix(anthropic): populate input_tokens in streaming usage metadata
body_text:
Fixes #35509

In ChatAnthropic streaming, `input_tokens` was always 0 because the value from Anthropic's `message_start` event was not being passed through to usage metadata. This PR correctly maps it.

entities.name: ChatAnthropic Streaming
entities.description: A streaming capability within the ChatAnthropic service.

entities.name: Input Tokens
entities.description: A metric representing the number of input tokens, which was incorrectly reported as 0.

entities.name: Anthropic Message Start Event
entities.description: An event from Anthropic's API that contains the correct input token count.

entities.name: Usage Metadata
entities.description: A system component or data structure where usage information is stored.

entities.name: Anthropic
entities.description: The organization providing the ChatAnthropic service.

relations.label: CAUSES
relations.evidence: the value from Anthropic's `message_start` event was not being passed through to usage metadata.

relations.label: RELATED_TO
relations.evidence: input_tokens was always 0 because the value from Anthropic's `message_start` event was not being passed through to usage metadata.

extracted_data.summary: The `input_tokens` metric in ChatAnthropic streaming was incorrectly reported as 0 because the value from Anthropic's `message_start` event was not being passed to usage metadata.
